free_text: Excitation functions for some W, Ta and Hf
radionuclides obtained by deuteron irradiation of
incident deuteron energy. 14 natural Ta foils with
interleaved monitor Ti foils of 12 microns at VUB
and Al degrader and monitor foils of 100 microns at
CYRIC. The monitor foils were used as recoil
catchers as well as. Beam current of 200 nA for 60
min (VUB) and 80 nA for 30 min (CYRIC), respectively.
Taking into account the cumulative effects of
possible incident energy variation and thickness of
the different targets, the uncertainty on the median
energy in the last foil was +-0.8 MeV for the
irradiation at VUB and +-0.9 MeV at CYRIC.
The uncertainty on cross-section was estimated
by taking the square root of the sum in quadrature
of all individual contributions, supposing equal
statistical error and peak area fit (5%);
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code ALICE-D,
The measured experimental data were compared to
Fitted excitation functions were used for

SF4: M->M2 in 007.  DECAY-DATA deleted in 001.
formation via decay of isomeric state.

both REACTION codes. SF8=SAT added to REACTION code (2)
SD: SF3=2N+P -> X, SF9=CALC -> DERIV in

Data for 40 MeV are estimated since no experimental
cross section data available above 20 MeV.
